title: [charizard] Battle Guide

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[charizard] is a classic [fire]/[flying] special sweeper that acts as the offensive engine of sun teams. [solar-power] converts its 109 SpA into a 160+ effective figure, and 100 Speed puts it ahead of the bulk of the unboosted metagame. Between nuclear [fire] STAB, flinch utility on [air-slash], and a one-turn [solar-beam], it can single-handedly crack open opposing back lines when given the right team support.

## Strengths
The [solar-power] + sun + [charcoal] stack produces one of the strongest [fire-blast] outputs in the format, cleanly two-shotting most defensive pivots. The [fire]/[flying] STAB combination covers Grass, Bug, Fairy, and [fighting]-resistant cores from useful angles, and the flinch roll on [air-slash] adds a probabilistic tempo lever that opponents cannot plan around. At 100 base Speed, [charizard] also raises the team's Speed tier floor, reducing the burden on dedicated Speed control.

## Weaknesses & Answers
stealth-rock is the single biggest issue — one uncleared set of rocks removes half of [charizard]'s HP on entry. Without a reliable hazard remover, the set simply should not be drafted. The 4x Rock weakness means one misclicked stone-edge ends the game on the spot, and Water or Electric attackers also pose an immediate threat. [solar-power] drains 1/8 HP per turn, which puts a hard clock on how long [charizard] can stay on the field.

## Recommended Moveset
[fire-blast] is the main STAB — stacked with [solar-power], sun, and [charcoal], it nukes even defensive threats in two hits. [air-slash] provides [flying] STAB and flinch pressure for slower bulky targets. [focus-blast] is the mandatory coverage slot that punishes Rock, Steel, and Dark switch-ins who try to wall the STAB combo. The last slot is [solar-beam], which one-turn-charges under sun and erases Water, Rock, and Ground pivots in a single click. On rainless teams, thunderbolt is an equally valid alternative.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[charcoal] is the default because its 20% [fire] multiplier multiplicatively stacks with [solar-power] and sun for a genuinely terrifying [fire-blast] number. [choice-scarf] is the alternative when sun uptime is unreliable, preserving the Speed axis. [focus-sash] is an insurance option against priority. The ability must be [solar-power]. For Tera, [fire] deepens [fire-blast]'s already absurd output; [fighting] is the defensive flip that removes the 4x Rock weakness outright.

## Teammates & Synergy
A dedicated sun setter plus a reliable stealth-rock remover (defog or rapid-spin) are effectively mandatory. Because [solar-power] wears [charizard] down quickly, it pairs well with status-spreading teammates that soften walls in advance — toxic or will-o-wisp users who chip opposing special tanks. Always lead with the support pivot and bring [charizard] in later through a safe switch.

## Play Patterns
Enter only after sun is up and stealth-rock has been removed. Once [charizard] drops below 50% HP, stop attacking and pivot out — staying in for a third turn usually means losing it to self-chip instead of a real trade.
